evidence.title: README (segment 2)
evidence.excerpt: > [!WARNING] > The Kyma CLI version `v2`, with all commands available within this version, is deprecated. Use the commands available as part of `v3` and contribute with use case ideas and general feedback.

evidence.title: README (segment 3)
evidence.excerpt: Kyma CLI is a command-line interface tool designed to simplify the use of [Kyma](https://github.com/kyma-project/kyma) for application developers. It helps to manage Kyma modules and deploy simple applications, automating complex tasks with simple commands and accelerating development cycles.

evidence.title: README (segment 4)
evidence.excerpt: For the installation instructions, see [How to Install](./docs/user/README.md#install-kyma-cli).

evidence.title: README (segment 6)
evidence.excerpt: The `my-first-kyma-app` application is available under the https://my-first-kyma-app.{CLUSTER_DOMAIN}/ address ```

evidence.title: 0.0.0-dev
evidence.excerpt: Release metadata available, release notes body is empty.

## What's Changed * 🍒 Fixed channels display for module catalog command (#2814) by @musztardem in https://github.com/kyma-project/cli/pull/2825 **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/kyma-project/cli/compare/3.3.0...3.3.1

## What's Changed ### Highlights Here are the highlights of the 3.3.0 release: #### Community module management improvements - Pull the latest community module templates (e.g., docker-registry) into the default namespace: ```bash kyma module pull docker-registry ``` - Install a community module from a pulled template (with a default CR): ```bash kyma module add default/docker-registry-0.10.0 --default-cr ``` - Remove an installed community module: ```bash kyma module delete default/docker-registry-0.10.0 ``` - Note: Behavior changes for community modules — see **Breaking Changes**. #### New “authorize” command group (alpha) - Grant access for users, groups, subaccounts, and GitHub repositories to your Kyma cluster in one step. - Discover options: ```bash kyma alpha authorize --help ``` #### Day 2 diagnostics (alpha) - Module/component error logs: ```bash kyma alpha diagnose logs --help ``` - Istio mesh configuration analysis (programmatic equivalent of istioctl analyze): ```bash kyma alpha diagnose istio --help ``` ### New Features 🚀 * Added kyma alpha authorize command by @musztardem in https://github.com/kyma-project/cli/pull/2655 * Revamp app push --mount flags by @Cortey in h…
